benchmarks.notes: 3 agents: Researcher/Coder/UIDeveloper. Mixed Python+C# output. All key deliverables present.
FAIL: Boss planned 1 trivial task (31-byte coder assignment). Coder produced no files. Final report was entirely hallucinated by boss. Staging dir empty.

PASS (no crash). csv_cleaner.py: all 4 cleaning ops implemented correctly. main.py: tkinter UI exists but uses hardcoded dummy data, no real CSV loading/saving, not integrated with csv_cleaner.py. Missing: sample.csv, README. Prior crash (Path:$.Handler serialization) fixed by inline JsonObject schema building in RunWorkerLoopAsync.

PASS (no crash, syntax check passed). Boss under-planned again (1 task: 'Execute goal') — same pattern as Combo B with gemma4:12b boss. Coder went off-task: produced a web scraper (scraper.py + requests/BeautifulSoup) instead of a CSV cleaner. main.py, README.md, requirements.txt present but wrong domain entirely. gemma4:12b as boss is consistently unsuitable for multi-task decomposition.

PASS (no crash). Best output after Combo A. csv_cleaner.py: Pandas-based, all 4 ops, type annotations, logging, proper error handling. main.py: fully integrated with csv_cleaner, tkinter Treeview preview, filedialog Load/Save, threading, disabled-until-ready save button. Missing: sample.csv, README. gemma4:12b as coder/researcher produced higher-quality code than smaller models when a good boss (qwen2.5-coder:14b) provides proper task decomposition.
